title: DL/NLP consolidation + DSA Graphs
focus: 60-min DL breadth quiz; rehearse self-attention, BERT, T5, transfer learning.

items.label: Self-attention(Q, K, V) end-to-end
interviewQuestions: Walk me through self-attention(Q, K, V) end-to-end.
What does multi-head attention buy you over a single head?

items.label: Encoder-decoder, positional encoding, residuals
interviewQuestions: Walk me through the transformer block: attention → add+norm → FFN → add+norm.
Compare absolute vs relative vs RoPE positional encodings.

items.label: Sparse, linear, FlashAttention, MQA, GQA
interviewQuestions: What problem does FlashAttention solve, and how?
Compare MHA, MQA, and GQA — KV-cache trade-offs.

items.label: BERT: MLM + NSP, encoder-only
interviewQuestions: Walk through BERT's MLM and NSP objectives.
Why is BERT bidirectional while GPT is left-to-right?
How would you fine-tune BERT for token classification (NER)?

items.label: T5 / BART: encoder-decoder
interviewQuestions: What is T5's text-to-text framing, and what does it enable?
Compare BART vs T5 for summarization.

items.label: GPT: decoder-only, causal LM
interviewQuestions: Why did decoder-only models win the LLM race?
What does the causal-LM objective give you that masked-LM doesn't, and vice versa?
Walk me through self-attention(Q, K, V) end-to-end including the √d_k scaling.
What does multi-head attention buy you over a single head?
Compare absolute, relative, and rotary (RoPE) positional encodings.
What problem does FlashAttention solve, and how does it speed up training?
Compare MHA vs MQA vs GQA — what changes in the KV cache?
